documents:
PCI-DSS v4.0.1 Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard.
Requirement 1: Install and maintain network security controls. Requirement 2: Apply secure configurations.
Requirement 3: Protect stored account data (encryption at rest AES-256). Requirement 4: Protect data in transit (TLS 1.2+).
Requirement 5: Protect against malicious software. Requirement 6: Develop secure systems.
Requirement 7: Restrict access by business need-to-know. Requirement 8: Identify users, authenticate access (MFA).
Req

8: Identify users, authenticate access (MFA).
Requirement 10: Log and monitor all access. Requirement 11: Test security regularly.
Requirement 12: Support infosec with organizational policies.

HIPAA Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act.
Security Rule: Administrative Safeguards (164.308) — risk analysis, workforce security, access management.
Physical Safeguards (164.310) — facility access controls, workstation security.
Technical Safeguards (164.312) — access control, audit controls, integrity, transmission security.
Required: encryption of ePHI at rest and in transit, access controls, audit logging, unique user IDs.

CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark v3.0. Identity and Access Management: avoid root account usage,
enable MFA for all IAM users, rotate access keys <90 days, enforce password policy.
Logging: enable CloudTrail in all regions, enable VPC Flow Logs, S3 access logging.
Monitoring: CloudWatch alarms for unauthorized API calls, root account usage, IAM policy changes.
Networking: restrict SSH/RDP to admin CIDRs, use VPC endpoints, disable default VPC usage.
Storage: enable S3 bucket encryption, enable vers
Storage: enable S3 bucket encryption, enable versioning, block public access.

CIS Azure Foundations Benchmark v2.0. Identity: enforce MFA for all users,
restrict Global Administrator count, disable guest user permissions.
Security Center: enable Microsoft Defender for all resource types, enable auto-provisioning.
Storage: require HTTPS, enable blob encryption, configure key rotation.
Networking: restrict RDP/SSH from internet, enable NSG flow logs, use Azure Firewall.
Monitoring: create activity log alerts for policy changes, enable diagnostic settings.
es, enable diagnostic settings.

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 Security and Privacy Controls.
AC (Access Control): AC-2 account management, AC-3 access enforcement, AC-6 least privilege.
AU (Audit): AU-2 event logging, AU-3 content of audit records, AU-6 audit review.
CM (Configuration): CM-2 baseline configuration, CM-6 configuration settings, CM-7 least functionality.
IA (Identification): IA-2 multi-factor authentication, IA-5 authenticator management.
SC (System Communications): SC-7 boundary protection, SC-8 transmission confidentia

boundary protection, SC-8 transmission confidentiality, SC-12 key management, SC-13 cryptographic protection, SC-28 protection of information at rest.
SI (System/Info Integrity): SI-2 flaw remediation, SI-3 malicious code protection, SI-4 system monitoring.

NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0. Functions: GOVERN, IDENTIFY, PROTECT, DETECT, RESPOND, RECOVER.
GV: Organizational Context, Risk Management Strategy, Roles and Responsibilities, Policy, Oversight.
ID: Asset Management, Business Environment, Risk Assessment, Supply Chain.
PR: Identity Management, Access Control, Data Security, Platform Security, Technology Infrastructure.
DE: Continuous Monitoring, Adverse Event Analysis.
RS: Incident Management, Analysis, Mitigation, Communications.
RC: Recove

, Analysis, Mitigation, Communications.
RC: Recovery Planning, Improvements, Communications.

ISO 27001:2022 Information Security Management System.
A.5 Organizational controls: policies, roles, asset classification, access control.
A.6 People controls: screening, awareness, remote working, reporting.
A.7 Physical controls: security perimeters, offices, equipment.
A.8 Technological controls: endpoint, access rights, privileged access, source code, configuration,
data masking, DLP, monitoring, web filtering, secure coding, network security, cryptography, logging.
, cryptography, logging.

SOC 2 Type II Trust Service Criteria.
Security (CC): CC6.1 logical access, CC6.3 role-based access, CC6.6 boundary protection,
CC6.7 data-in-transit, CC6.8 malicious software prevention, CC7.2 system monitoring.
Availability (A): A1.2 recovery mechanisms, A1.3 backup procedures.
Processing Integrity (PI): PI1.3 data processing accuracy.
Confidentiality (C): C1.1 confidential data identification, C1.2 disposal.
Privacy (P): P1-P8 privacy criteria for personal information.
for personal information.

GDPR General Data Protection Regulation (EU 2016/679).
Article 5: Principles — lawfulness, purpose limitation, data minimisation, accuracy, storage limitation.
Article 25: Data protection by design and by default. Article 32: Security of processing —
encryption, pseudonymisation, ensure confidentiality/integrity/availability, ability to restore.
Article 33: Breach notification within 72 hours. Article 35: Data protection impact assessment.

SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act. Section 302: CEO/CFO certification of financial reports.
Section 404: Management assessment of internal controls over financial reporting (ICFR).
IT General Controls: access management, change management, IT operations, programming controls.
Requires: segregation of duties, access reviews, audit trails, change management procedures.

FISMA Federal Information Security Management Act. Requires federal agencies to implement
information security programs. Based on NIST SP 800-53 controls. Requires: risk categorization (FIPS 199),
security control selection, continuous monitoring, annual assessment, POA&M tracking.

FedRAMP Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program.
Cloud service authorization for US government. Based on NIST 800-53 High/Moderate/Low baselines.
Requires: ConMon (Continuous Monitoring), POA&M tracking, 3PAO assessment, encryption (FIPS 140-2),
multi-factor authentication, vulnerability scanning, incident response within 1 hour.

CCPA/CPRA California Consumer Privacy Act/California Privacy Rights Act.
Rights: know what personal information is collected, delete personal information, opt-out of PII sale.
Requires: reasonable security procedures, data inventory, privacy impact assessments,
consumer access request handling within 45 days, breach notification.
Compliance framework: aws_waf
Compliance framework: azure_security

NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2: Protecting CUI in Nonfederal Systems.
3.1 Access Control: limit system access, control CUI flow, separate duties, least privilege.
3.3 Audit: create, protect, retain audit records. 3.5 Identification: identify and authenticate users with MFA.
3.13 System/Comm: monitor and protect communications at boundaries, implement cryptographic mechanisms.
Compliance framework: csa_ccm
Compliance framework: cis_docker
Compliance framework: cis_kubernetes
Compliance framework: mitre_attack
Compliance framework: ens
Compliance framework: glba
Compliance framework: ferpa

CMMC v2.0 Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification.
Level 1 (Foundational): 17 practices based on FAR 52.204-21.
Level 2 (Advanced): 110 practices aligned with NIST SP 800-171.
Level 3 (Expert): 134+ practices based on NIST SP 800-172.
Requires: access control, awareness training, audit, configuration management, IA, incident response,
maintenance, media protection, physical protection, personnel security, risk assessment, security assessment,
system/comm protection, system/info integrity.
system/comm protection, system/info integrity.
